summary: Review #5 of CC-0013 changes addressing greptile-apps[bot] review #4 feedback. Four targeted fixes: (1) CacheSpec.Replicas field replaces hardcoded 3-replica topology assumption, (2) ConfigMap name prefixed with keystone.Name for multi-instance support, (3) buildDatabase/buildGrant now use spec.database.database instead of metadata.name, (4) CronJob env var OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys passes max_active_keys to fernet_rotate. All tests pass, go vet clean, golangci-lint 0 issues.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... — all pass)
Edge cases covered (custom replicas, CR name != DB name, min keys floor)
New test TestReconcileConfig_ManagedCacheCustomReplicas verifies Replicas=5 generates 5 endpoints
New test DatabaseAndGrantUseSpecDatabaseName verifies spec.database.database used (not metadata.name)
Updated TestReconcileFernetKeys_CronJobSpec verifies 3 env vars including OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys

code_quality_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and focused on review feedback
Defensive fallback in resolveCacheServers (replicas <= 0 → 3) matches kubebuilder default
ConfigMap naming now consistent with other resources (keystone.Name prefix)

security_checklist.item: No new user inputs exposed without validation
Replicas field has kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1 + runtime guard
Fernet key generation still uses crypto/rand
No secrets exposed in env vars (OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys is an integer config value)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
CacheSpec.Replicas in shared types package is appropriate (used by config generation)
oslo.config env var convention (OS_<GROUP>__<KEY>) is standard OpenStack pattern

fail_fast_checklist.item: Defensive replicas <= 0 check prevents invalid endpoint generation

defensive_checklist.item: Replicas field: kubebuilder marker (Minimum=1) + default (3) + runtime guard (<=0 → 3)
